task: Launch-quality advanced task: Water Reuse Salinity Blending Optimizer. Reality context: a water reuse plant must blend streams while protecting membranes and crops. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=tds_mg_L, metric2=flow_m3_h, metric3=boron_mg_L, metric4=membrane_recovery_pct; records: R1 tds_mg_L=809 flow_m3_h=105 boron_mg_L=0.63 membrane_recovery_pct=67.8; R2 tds_mg_L=860 flow_m3_h=223 boron_mg_L=2.5 membrane_recovery_pct=49.4; R3 tds_mg_L=1618 flow_m3_h=154 boron_mg_L=3.95 membrane_recovery_pct=47.6; R4 tds_mg_L=1698 flow_m3_h=248 boron_mg_L=0.59 membrane_recovery_pct=82.7; limit_tds_mg_L=2421; limit_flow_m3_h=21; limit_boron_mg_L=3.95; decision_budget_usd=205000; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="benchmark framing favored long-horizon professional work with concrete verification". Rules and constraints: Treat confidence below 0.70, missing provenance, or contradictory record status as needs_review even when the numeric score passes. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: compute compliant blend and recovery limits; convert the packet into an auditable evidence table, cite the governing values, and list unresolved assumptions. Deliver salinity_blending_optimizer.xlsx + ops_setpoint.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
